exemplar.title:
Investigating the volume and mass of a violin, then finding the density in order to
determine what type of wood it is made of.

detailedExemplarData.title:
Investigating the volume and mass of a violin, then finding the density in order to
determine what type of wood it is made of.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear investigative thread with logical progression from introduction through modelling to conclusion
Well-defined structure using headings, sub-headings, table of contents and page numbers
Figures, tables and appendices are sensibly placed to support the narrative
weaknesses: Minor breaks in flow when narrative interrupts calculations or information is repeated
Some sections lack sub-section breaks and summaries, reducing navigational ease
Extended narrative on acoustics and repeated data dilute conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands are rated 3 (coherent, logically developed; well organized) and 2 (conciseness somewhat diluted). These combine to an overall score of 3 out of 4, reflecting good structure and coherence but some unnecessary or repetitive content.

strengths: Consistent and mostly accurate use of mathematical notation and symbols throughout
Effective use of multiple representations (GeoGebra graphs, hand sketches, tables) linked to the discussion
Logical, well-ordered presentation of calculations with units carried through and summary statements
weaknesses: Occasional slips in notation (e.g. missing âdxâ in integrals, inconsistent variable names)
Some diagrams (frustum sketch, Table 2 graphs) lack scales or axis labels
Compressed steps in trigonometric integrals create minor gaps in clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand assessments sit at band 3, the studentâs mostly consistent notation, effective multi- representations and clear logical communication justify elevating to a 4 out of 4 for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Rationale vividly connects personal experience to the mathematical investigation, showing genuine ownership
Personal measurements and justifications of assumptions demonstrate clear individual involvement
Proposal of matrix transformations and choice of a real violin indicate strong initiative and creativity
weaknesses: Geometric simplifications (cones, frusta) follow standard approaches rather than wholly original methods
Mathematical decisions largely reflect conventional modelling choices
Personal approach, while strong, stops short of exceptional innovation in derivation techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of significant independent thinking and a highly personal approach support awarding the maximum 3 out of 3 for Personal Engagement, recognising the studentâs strong ownership and creativity.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement accuracy, model assumptions and external factors
Thoughtful analysis of error sources such as rib thickness, f-holes omission and averaging densities
Clear evaluation comparing calculated density with literature values
weaknesses: Reflection lacks quantitative treatment of uncertainty or confidence intervals
Critical analysis is competent but not deeply insightful or sustained throughout
Future implications and propagation of assumptions into results are not comprehensively addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands align with band 2 across depth of reflection, critical analysis and evaluation. The reflection is meaningful but not substantial or critically deep, meriting 2 out of 3.

strengths: Relevant calculus, solid geometry and unit conversions are used effectively to meet the investigationâs aim
Mathematics is fully commensurate with SL level, including challenging integrals and 3-D volume calculations
Derivations are sound and manual and GDC results concur, demonstrating solid understanding
weaknesses: Minor notation errors (ellipse and sphere formula typos, inconsistent significant figures)
Compressed arithmetic steps in some integrations obscure intermediate reasoning
Small domain boundary and volume subtraction precision issues require careful checking
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand assessments sit at band 4, the breadth and effective application of SL-level mathematicsâwith only minor inaccuraciesâjustify a 5 out of 6 for Use of Mathematics, reflecting comprehensive and well-applied mathematical content.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are set out in a logical order and most steps are explained.  Units are carried through calculations and results are gathered in summary statements.  Some working (e.g., evaluation of trigonometric integral limits) is compressed, causing minor gaps in clarity, so the descriptor âgood logical structureâ is suitable.

Derivations and calculator checks show solid understanding; results from manual and GDC integration coincide.  Minor inaccuracies (square-root translation error, inconsistent significant figures) prevent the award of the top two bands, but understanding is good and work is mostly correct.

The student combines algebraic functions, graphs from GeoGebra, tables of measurements and hand sketches of 3-D solids.  These representations are generally well linked to the discussion and aid comprehension.  A small number of diagrams (e.g., frustum sketch) lack scales, limiting complete effectiveness.

The report follows a clear investigative thread (introduction â modelling â calculations â conclusion) and the mathematical arguments generally build on one another.  Minor breaks in flow occur when descriptive narrative interrupts calculations or when identical information is repeated (e.g., repeated description of functions), preventing the work from reaching the highest level of seamless development.

Modelling complex parts of the violin with cones, frusta and trapezoids shows creativity and initiative.  However, these are classical geometric simplifications and do not constitute exceptional ingenuity; therefore the middle band is most appropriate.

There is a thoughtful analysis of sources of error (rib thickness, neglecting f-holes, averaging ebony densities) and the likely impact on results.  The analysis is competent but not âdeep and insightfulâ; uncertainties are listed rather than quantitatively explored.

Outcomes (density 0.668 g cmâ»Â³) are compared with literature values and limitations are discussed.  No numeric uncertainty or confidence interval is provided, so the evaluation is clear but not comprehensive with future implications, fitting band 2.

The student reflects on measurement accuracy, modelling assumptions and external factors affecting density, demonstrating meaningful reflection throughout.  Reflection is present in relevant sections but not developed into sustained critical commentary that permeates the whole work.

Appropriate mathematical symbols (â«, Ï, cmÂ³, density = mass/volume, etc.) are used consistently and variables are usually defined.  There are occasional slips (e.g., âA_Cylinderâ instead of âV_Cylinderâ, missing differential âdxâ in some integrals), so the use is âmostly consistentâ rather than perfectly accurate.

The student clearly owns the investigation: measurements are taken personally, assumptions are justified, and reflection sections are written in the first-person perspective.  While the approach is personalised, it is not especially novel or original, corresponding to âclear evidence of personal approachâ.

Most material is relevant to establishing the violinâs density; however, extended narrative on violin acoustics, screenshots of basic graphs and lengthy quotations dilute conciseness.  The exploration often repeats measurement data and calculator screenshots, indicating some unnecessary content.

Calculus for area, solid geometry, unit conversions and mean calculations are all directly relevant to the aim and integrated coherently.  Although some sections (e.g., averaging ebony density) are simplistic, the mathematics as a whole is undoubtedly relevant and used effectively.

The topic stems from the studentâs personal experience and the decision to model a real violin shows significant independent thinking.  Nonetheless, most mathematical decisions (choice of elementary functions, use of averages) follow standard approaches rather than innovative derivations, placing the work in the âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ category.

Headings, sub-headings, a table of contents and page numbers give the exploration a recognisable structure.  Figures, tables and appendices are sensibly placed.  Some sections (particularly the very long Section II) would benefit from sub-section breaks and a short summary, so the organisation is judged âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ.

The calculus and 3-D geometry deployed are fully commensurate with SL expectations.  A few formulae (volume of frustum, ellipse area) exceed syllabus but are applied in straightforward ways.  Overall the level is appropriate and fully meets course standards, hence band 4.

annotations.comment: The ellipse area is given by \(A=\pi ab\), not \((a\times b)\pi\) with hyphens. Use precise notation: \(A=\pi ab\).
The frustum volume formula should be \(V=\tfrac h3(A_1+\sqrt{A_1A_2}+A_2)\). Clarify each term in your notation.
The sphere volume formula was miswritten as \(V=\frac43\pi r^2\). It should read \(V=\tfrac43\pi r^3\).
The trapezium area formula is \(A=\tfrac{h}{2}(a+b)\). Your notation \((h/2)(side-a+side-b)\) may confuse readers.
Averaging densities from diverse ebony species treats them equally; consider quantifying uncertainty or selecting the most probable species.
The tabulated masses of tailpiece and strings are presented clearly, aiding transparency in the mass budget.
The title clearly identifies the standard level and internal assessment but would benefit from a succinct research question that frames the investigationâs scope and purpose.
The opening rationale is concise but underdeveloped. Expand on how density specifically influences tonal characteristics to strengthen relevance.
Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers, reducing navigational clarity. Ensure each section heading is linked to its page.
The rationale section vividly connects personal experience to mathematical inquiry, showing genuine curiosity about the relationship between wood and sound.
The extended narrative on spruce acoustics, while informative, interrupts the mathematical thread. Consider relocating detailed acoustics discussion to the reflection.
The aim of investigation truncates mid-sentence, leaving the plan unclear. Complete the statement to outline each step of density determination.
The dimensions table is comprehensive, but column headings mix âWideâ and âWidthâ. Standardize terminology for consistency.
Overlaying the photo in GeoGebra and reducing its opacity is a clear and effective multiple representation strategy.
Table 2 lacks scale bars on the plotted graphs. Add axis labels and scales to improve interpretability and precision.

You clearly justify choosing \(g(x)=7.963\,
\sin(0.14x+0.56)-0.3\) for manual integration, demonstrating critical selection of a challenging example.
In the manual integral \(\int (\sqrt{18x})\,dx\), the differential \(dx\) is omitted. Always include differentials in integral notation.
The manual integration of \(g(x)\) compresses the arithmetic step. Show intermediate cosine evaluations to aid reader understanding.
The integral for \(k(x)\) has matching limits \(x=31.6\) to \(34.17\) yielding zero width. Check domain boundaries for this segment.
When translating the exterior curves down by 0.2 cm, confirm the algebraic form of \(l(x)=\sqrt{18x}-0.2\) is consistent with your innerâface graph.
Subtracting interior from exterior volume with consistent significant figures is crucial. Retain matching precision (e.g. 4 sig. fig.) throughout.
The cylinder volume formula uses \(A_{Cylinder}\) to denote crossâsectional area; rename to \(V_{base}\) or \(A_{base}\) to avoid confusion.
Using cones to approximate the scroll demonstrates initiative, but please justify how cone volumes closely approximate the true spiral shape.
The parallelogram and cuboid calculations for the pegbox are clear, showing effective use of SLâlevel geometry.
The calculation \(328 - (61.84+63.59)=202.57\)g uses 3 sig. fig. for mass but 5 sig. fig. for volume; align significant figures for consistency.
Comparing final density to spruce table strongly supports Norwegian spruce identification, reflecting effective use of quantitative analysis.
The conclusion restates results but lacks discussion of how assumptions propagated into uncertainty. Add critical evaluation of confidence in your density.
The reflection on modelling inaccuracies and omission of f-holes shows thoughtful analysis of sources of error.
Proposing matrix transformations to refine curve fitting demonstrates innovative thinking and strong personal engagement.
The bibliography uses varied formats and incomplete URLs. Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) and include full access details.
